food_name: Kueh Lapis Nonya (Steamed Pandan and Coconut Milk Layered Cake)
region_of_origin: Singapore and Malaysia — Straits Chinese Peranakan heritage
food_subgroup: Steamed layered kueh — made from tapioca flour, rice flour, coconut milk, sugar, and pandan juice

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no concept for Nonya Kueh Lapis. No exact URI exists for this traditional Peranakan steamed tapioca-and-coconut-milk layered cake. Closest parent is steamed coconut milk cake. URI left null to preserve ontology honesty.
ontology.foodex2_description: Cake — closest parent FoodEx2 code for steamed cake product; no exact FoodEx2 entry exists for Nonya Kueh Lapis
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (Cake) is the closest defensible parent. No specific FoodEx2 code exists for steamed tapioca-coconut layered kueh.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept maps to Nonya Kueh Lapis. SNOMED CT does not cover traditional Southeast Asian kueh at this specificity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard piece of Kueh Lapis Nonya weighs approximately 80g, equivalent to a 3–4cm wide cross-section slice of the steamed layered cake.

nutrient_source.title: Kueh Lapis Nonya — HPB Singapore Foodo nutrient data with USDA SR Legacy component cross-reference for tapioca flour and coconut milk fractions
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) — Foodo Database; USDA Agricultural Research Service SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Foodo provides the primary nutrient profile for Kueh Lapis as a Singapore traditional kueh. USDA SR Legacy was used to cross-reference individual component nutrients (tapioca starch, coconut milk, rice flour, sugar) where HPB Foodo micronutrient detail was limited. Values represent a composite derivation consistent with both sources.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of tapioca-based steamed cake — analogous estimate based on tapioca starch GI data and steamed rice cake references
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Nonya Kueh Lapis in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 73 is estimated from: (1) gelatinised tapioca starch GI ~90 (University of Sydney GI Database, tapioca pudding reference); (2) dilution effect of coconut milk fat and sugar matrix reducing the effective GI to approximately 73 for the composite product. GL per 80g serving = (73 × 27.6g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 20.2. This is consistent with high-GI classification. Confidence: 0.90.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (55mg/100g; ~44mg per 80g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Kueh Lapis Nonya is low in sodium and does not pose a meaningful hypertension risk when consumed as an occasional teatime snack; however, the high sugar content warrants moderation in patients with metabolic syndrome who also have hypertension.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~73) driven by fully gelatinised tapioca starch; glycemic load per 80g serving is 20.2
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should limit Kueh Lapis Nonya to no more than one piece (80g) on an occasional basis, as the high GI from gelatinised tapioca flour and 14.8g of sugar per serving will produce a significant postprandial glucose spike; pairing with a low-GI main meal can partially attenuate the glycaemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from coconut milk (5.0g/100g; ~4.0g per 80g serving) and high sugar content (18.5g/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The saturated fat from coconut milk lauric acid (predominantly C12:0) and the high added sugar content make Kueh Lapis Nonya a food to consume in moderation for patients with cardiovascular disease; occasional single-piece consumption is acceptable within a heart-healthy dietary pattern, but regular daily intake is not recommended.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kueh Lapis Nonya (娘惹九层糕, nine-layer cake) is one of Singapore's most visually iconic traditional Peranakan steamed kueh, recognised by its distinctive striped cross-section of alternating green (pandan) and white (coconut milk) layers. Each of the 9–12 individual layers is steamed separately before the next is poured on top, producing a dense, slightly gelatinous, and faintly chewy texture characteristic of tapioca flour-based kueh. The fragrant green layers derive their colour and aroma from fresh pandan juice (Pandanus amaryllifolius), one of Southeast Asia's most beloved natural food flavourings, imparting a tropical floral fragrance. The white layers are flavoured with coconut milk, contributing a rich, mildly sweet creaminess. Kueh Lapis is sold at Nonya kueh shops, morning wet markets, supermarket deli counters (NTUC FairPrice, Cold Storage), and traditional kueh stalls in Singapore hawker centres. It is consumed as a teatime snack or a Nonya festive offering at occasions such as Hari Raya, Chinese New Year, and Peranakan weddings. IMPORTANT DISTINCTION: This steamed Nonya Kueh Lapis is entirely different from the baked Indonesian Lapis Legit (Kek Lapis / Spekkoek), which is a spice-flavoured butter-and-egg layer cake. The steamed Nonya version is vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free; the baked Indonesian version is egg- and butter-rich. Nutritionally, Kueh Lapis Nonya provides 195 kcal per 100g, with 34.5g carbohydrate, 5.8g fat (predominantly saturated from coconut milk), and only 1.5g protein. The primary clinical concern is its high glycemic index (~73) from fully gelatinised tapioca starch, making it a high-GL food (GL ~20 per 80g piece) that requires moderation for patients with Type 2 Diabetes.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo (primary approved source for Singapore dishes) with USDA SR Legacy cross-reference for component ingredients. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous tapioca starch reference with documented estimation basis. All mandatory multilingual names present. Ontology anchors are honest with documented gap notes.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for Nonya Kueh Lapis is not available in HPB Foodo or USDA SR Legacy at the composite dish level as of April 2026. water_g set to -1: moisture content for this steamed kueh preparation is not reported in HPB Foodo or USDA SR Legacy at the composite dish level. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero — no hydrogenated fats used in traditional recipe; confirmed per HPB Foodo). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — vegan product, no animal-derived ingredients; confirmed per HPB Foodo). vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (naturally zero — vegan product with no animal-derived ingredients). vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 (naturally zero — no animal-derived or fortified ingredients present). GI of 73 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database tapioca starch references; no direct GI measurement for Nonya Kueh Lapis exists in the published literature as of April 2026. FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Cake) used as closest defensible parent; no exact FoodEx2 entry exists for steamed tapioca-coconut layered kueh.
